en: When sports were large sizes prized the large athletes have gotten larger While indeed we haven't evolved into a new species in
a century the gene pool within competitive sports most certainly has changed Today rather than the same size as
the average elite high jumper the average elite shot putter is two and a half inches taller and 130 pounds heavier
And this happened throughout the sports world In 1983 the National Basketball Association signed a groundbreaking agreement
making players partners in the league entitled to shares of ticket revenues and television contracts
Suddenly anybody who could be an NBA player wanted to be and teams started scouring the globe for the bodies that could help them
the proportion of men in the NBA who are at least seven feet tall doubled to 10 percent
Today one in 10 men in the NBA are at least seven feet tall But a seven foot tall man is incredibly
rare in the general population So rare that if you know an American man between the ages of 20 and 40 who's
at least seven feet tall there's a 17 percent chance he's in the NBA right now
